title: mqobsidian status-manifest.v1
description: Canonical current-status surface for the MQ stack: one exported manifest that answers 'what is the state of each repo right now?' as a read, not a computation. mqobsidian owns this contract under the single-source-of-truth model (ROADMAP Delivery A/D; see DEC-002); consumers (mqlaunch read-only, mq-agent orchestration) read it and never reconstruct current status from raw evidence. This tightens the loose stack-truth.v1 export into a typed, closed shape. freshness_state uses the canonical freshness axis (experimental/active/stale/deprecated) — deliberately distinct from the promotion axis used by memory-score.v1 / promotion-event.v1.

schema.description: Schema discriminator. Consumers must match this exact value before trusting the shape.

source.description: Producer identity that generated this manifest (e.g. an mqobsidian export job). mqobsidian is the sole producer.

generated_at.description: When this manifest was materialized. The primary freshness marker consumers compare against staleness thresholds (Delivery D).

repos.description: One status record per tracked repo. The array is the whole current-status surface; consumers read it directly.

name.description: Canonical repo key (e.g. macos-scripts, mq-agent). Stable identifier; not a display label.

hot.description: Vault-relative path to the repo's hot-context note, if one is exported. Never absolute; resolved under the consumer's vault root.

freshness_state.description: Current freshness on the canonical freshness axis. NOT the promotion axis (observed/candidate/promoted/deprecated/archived) — the two coexist by design.

drift.description: True when the exported truth for this repo is known to lag its source (a drift marker, Delivery D). Consumers may warn but must still read; absence means no drift asserted.

last_verified_at.description: When this repo's status was last confirmed against source. Distinct from generated_at (which times the manifest, not the per-repo check).

summary.description: Short human-readable status line for menus/dashboards only; carries no machine meaning.
